food_subgroup: Noodle soup dish — herbal broth ramen with mushroom and fried dough

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists for Bak Kut Teh Ramen specifically. FOODON_00002700 (noodle dish) is the closest defensible parent category.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0CKP (Noodle soup) is the closest parent. No specific code exists for Bak Kut Teh Ramen or lion's mane mushroom ramen fusion.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code exists for this specific fusion dish. SNOMED CT does not cover prepared ethnic noodle soup dishes at this specificity.

main_ingredients: black bak kut teh herbal broth (dark soy sauce, herbs, spices, pork bones or vegetarian base)
Chinese herbs (dang gui, goji berry, star anise, cinnamon, cloves)

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard restaurant serving is approximately 550 g, comprising a bowl of ramen noodles in black herbal bak kut teh broth with lion's mane mushroom and one or two pieces of you tiao (fried dough stick) on the side.

nutrient_source.title: Estimated from component analysis: Bak Kut Teh (HPB Singapore Foodo), Ramen noodle soup (USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy #21156), You Tiao / Chinese cruller (USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy #18617), Lion's Mane Mushroom (USDA FoodData Central Foundation Foods)
nutrient_source.organization: Health Promotion Board Singapore (HPB) / USDA FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No single approved-source entry exists for this specific fusion dish. Nutrient values are estimated by weighted component analysis: approximately 60% black bak kut teh broth with noodles (HPB Foodo BKT reference + USDA ramen noodle soup), 25% you tiao contribution (USDA SR Legacy), and 15% lion's mane mushroom (USDA Foundation Foods). Per-100g values are derived from the blended 550g serving estimate. Confidence is medium due to estimation.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of noodle soups and mixed Asian noodle dishes — analogous estimate based on ramen/noodle soup GI data (GI ~50–55) from the University of Sydney GI Database; you tiao GI estimated at ~76 weighted by serving proportion
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Lion's Mane Black Bak Kut Teh Ramen with You Tiao. GI estimated as weighted composite: ramen noodle soup GI ~52 (University of Sydney GI Database, wheat noodle soup analogues) weighted at ~75% of carbohydrate load; you tiao (Chinese fried dough) GI ~76 weighted at ~25% of carbohydrate load. Blended GI ≈ 52. Glycemic load calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g_per_serving) / 100 = (52 × 62.7) / 100 ≈ 32.5 for a 550g serving (carbohydrate_g per serving = 11.4 × 5.5 = 62.7g).

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content from herbal broth and dark soy sauce
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit or avoid this dish due to its high sodium content (approximately 420 mg per 100g, equating to ~2,310 mg per 550g serving), which approaches or exceeds the daily recommended sodium limit of 2,000 mg; requesting reduced-sodium broth or consuming only a portion of the soup is advised.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate glycemic load from wheat ramen noodles and you tiao
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should be cautious with this dish as the wheat ramen noodles and deep-fried you tiao contribute a moderate-to-high glycemic load (~32.5 GL per serving); portion control, substituting with lower-GI noodles, and limiting you tiao intake can help manage postprandial blood glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Elevated sodium and saturated fat from broth and fried dough
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should moderate consumption of this dish due to its elevated sodium content and saturated fat contribution from the you tiao and broth; the lion's mane mushroom component provides beta-glucan dietary fibre which may offer modest cardiovascular benefit, but overall sodium and fat levels warrant portion control.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Lion's Mane Mushroom Black Bak Kut Teh Ramen with You Tiao is a Singaporean-Japanese fusion noodle dish that combines the rich, dark herbal broth of black bak kut teh (肉骨茶, Hei Rou Gu Cha) with Japanese-style ramen noodles and lion's mane mushroom (Hericium erinaceus), served alongside you tiao (油条), the traditional Chinese deep-fried dough stick. Black bak kut teh is distinguished from the lighter Teochew-style by its use of dark soy sauce, giving the broth a deep, umami-rich, slightly sweet and intensely savoury character with aromatic Chinese herbs including dang gui (angelica root), star anise, cinnamon, cloves, and goji berries. The lion's mane mushroom adds a meaty, seafood-like texture reminiscent of crab or lobster, and contributes beta-glucan dietary fibre with potential neuroprotective and immune-modulating properties. The ramen noodles absorb the herbal broth, providing a chewy, satisfying base, while the you tiao adds a crispy, airy contrast when dipped into the soup. This dish is served as a main meal and is popular in modern Singaporean fusion restaurants. Nutritionally, it provides moderate protein (~5.2g/100g), moderate carbohydrates (~11.4g/100g), and is notably high in sodium (~420mg/100g) due to the soy-based broth. The glycemic index is estimated at approximately 52 (low-medium range), with a moderate glycemic load per serving. Clinically, the high sodium content is a concern for hypertension and cardiovascular disease management, while the moderate glycemic load requires portion awareness for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes. The dish is dairy-free and contains gluten from wheat noodles and you tiao.

confidence_reason: No single approved-source entry exists for this specific fusion dish. Nutrient values are estimated by weighted component analysis using HPB Singapore Foodo (bak kut teh reference), USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (ramen noodle soup, you tiao), and USDA Foundation Foods (lion's mane mushroom). GI is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database noodle soup data. Confidence is medium due to the composite estimation approach and the novel fusion nature of the dish.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are estimated from component analysis as no direct approved-source entry exists for this specific dish. Values should be treated as indicative and updated if a direct HPB or USDA entry becomes available. 2. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the component source databases for this dish type. 3. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the component source databases for this dish type. 4. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the component source databases for this dish type. 5. trans_fat_g is 0.0 — confirmed zero or negligible per USDA SR Legacy data for the component foods. 6. GI is an analogous estimate (GI ~52) based on wheat noodle soup analogues from the University of Sydney GI Database; no direct GI measurement exists for this fusion dish. 7. The food_group has been corrected from the input value of 'Beverages' to 'Mixed' as this is a noodle soup main dish, not a beverage. 8. Halal status is marked false as the dish may contain pork-derived broth depending on restaurant preparation; the specific restaurant (Flavours by Saute - Funan) does not appear to hold a MUIS halal certification. 9. Serving size of 550g is an estimate for a standard restaurant bowl with you tiao; actual serving size may vary by restaurant.
